# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.180.0/http/server.ts";

const ALLOWED_MODULES = [
  "https://deno.land/std@0.180.0/fmt/colors.ts",
  "https://deno.land/std@0.180.0/collections/mod.ts",
  "https://deno.land/std@0.180.0/async/delay.ts",
];

function validateModulePath(modulePath: string): boolean {
  try {
    const url = new URL(modulePath);
    return ALLOWED_MODULES.some(allowed => 
      url.origin === new URL(allowed).origin
    );
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function sanitizeInput(input: string): string {
  return input.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9\-_\/.]/g, '');
}

async function loadModule(modulePath: string) {
  if (!validateModulePath(modulePath)) {
    throw new Error("Module not allowed");
  }
  
  try {
    const module = await import(modulePath);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to load module: ${error.message}`);
  }
}

serve(async (req: Request) => {
  try {
    const url = new URL(req.url);
    const modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module") || 
                      Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH") || 
                      "";
    
    if (!modulePath) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Module path required" }),
        { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
      );
    }
    
    const sanitizedPath = sanitizeInput(modulePath);
    
    if (!sanitizedPath) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid module path" }),
        { status: 400, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
      );
    }
    
    const module = await loadModule(sanitizedPath);
    
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ 
        success: true, 
        module: sanitizedPath,
        exports: Object.keys(module)
      }),
      { headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: error.message }),
      { status: 500, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
    );
  }
});
